svg.text: mHC 工程实现 · fused kernel + selective recompute + DualPipe overlap
① fused mhc_pre / mhc_post kernel
inputs : x [T, n_hc=4, d=7168] bf16
: hc_fn [mix_hc=24, n_hc·d=28672] float32 · hc_scale [3] · hc_base [24]
stages : RMSNorm(flatten) → dynamic A/B/C gen → Sinkhorn 20 iters → mix
outputs : layer_input [T, d], post_mix [T, n_hc, d], res_mix [T, n_hc, d]
• single kernel ⇒ no intermediate materialization
• training & inference share the same CUDA/TileLang kernel (byte-identical)
② Selective recompute (tensor-level checkpointing)
recompute : inter-layer hidden state [T, d=7168]
: RMSNorm outputs [T, d]
retain : wq/wkv GEMM outputs (compute-heavy)
: compressor intermediates
activation memory: n_hc=4 inflates 4× if naive
after selective recompute: extra footprint ≤ 10%
③ DualPipe 1F1B schedule tuned for mHC extra traffic
conventional 1F1B: fwd[i] → comm → bwd[i] · mHC adds n_hc× residual traffic between pipeline stages
V4 change 1: some mHC ops executed concurrently with adjacent compute — hc_pre of stage i+1 overlaps with hc_post of i
V4 change 2: send buffers grouped by n_hc slab so a single NVLink packet carries multiple mHC channels
wall-time overhead of mHC in production pipeline: 6.7%
for reference: naive residual adds zero comm → mHC's 6.7% is the whole cost of going from R^d to R^(n_hc·d)
see dedicated mHC paper (Xie et al., 2026) for engineering appendix
